subject: Mozi Minute: Start Stop Keep
body:
Words I like: Most managers tell people who to be. Great managers tell people what to do.

Mozi Minute: Start Stop Keep Method

Every time I hear a manager say "be more proactive" or "show more leadership," I cringe.

Those words mean nothing.

Here's the framework that works:

Start. Stop. Keep.

Tell people exactly what actions you want.

No personality traits. No vague feedback. Pure action items.

Example:

Instead of "be more assertive," say "START speaking in the first 5 minutes of every meeting."

Instead of "be less of a dick," say "STOP interrupting people when they're presenting, take notes instead and ask if you can give feedback when they've finished."

The formula is simple:

- START doing [specific action]
- STOP doing [specific action]
- KEEP doing [specific action]

Make no mistake, it's harder to be specific.

But my mental hack for figuring out what I actually want them to do is to think of the *moment* I felt someone was lacking.

That *moment* came from an action they did or didn't take.

Then I just relay that back to them.

Ex: Why do I think Erica is lazy?

The *moment*: I messaged her last week and she didn't get back to me for 30min during work time and her calendar was empty.

→ Feedback: START: "I need you to turn on notifications and respond within 5min during working hours if not on a meeting."

That's it.

Fast clear feedback everyday until you retire. I wish I were kidding.

But that's the game. The only thing that changes, is who you give feedback to.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Clarity creates compliance,

Alex
